title: DORA TLPT — Tester Independence and Conflict-of-Interest Attestation Log

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.5 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.5 (ICT risk-management governance) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.3.26: DORA TLPT — Tester Independence and Conflict-of-Interest Attestation Log.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.26' running on index=grc sourcetype="tlpt:independence" (tester_id, engagement_id, attestation_type, signed_date, conflict_flag), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: HTTP Event Collector from HR/GRC web form
spl:
index=grc sourcetype="tlpt:independence" earliest=-400d conflict_flag=true
| stats latest(signed_date) as last_signed, values(attestation_type) as types by tester_id, engagement_id
| sort tester_id
description: Independence of testers from operational teams is a supervisory expectation. A tamper-evident log of tester employer changes, asset holdings declarations, and recusal events supports governance of TLPT quality.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for DORA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes tlpt:independence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Require attestation before VPN tokens issued; (2) integrate MSSP tester roster; (3) alert on `conflict_flag=true` until cleared by legal; (4) retain 7 years per records policy; (5) hash payloads for integrity. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=tlpt:independence`.
visualization: Table (conflicts), Single value (open conflicts), Pie by attestation_type. Key fields: `engagement_id`, `last_signed`, `tester_id`, `types`.
knownFalsePositives: Incidents during planned ICT changes, controlled DR drills, or after major upgrade waves.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: HTTP Event Collector from HR/GRC web form.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` `sourcetype="tlpt:independence"` (tester_id, engagement_id, attestation_type, signed_date, conflict_flag).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Require attestation before VPN tokens issued; (2) integrate MSSP tester roster; (3) alert on `conflict_flag=true` until cleared by legal; (4) retain 7 years per records policy; (5) hash payloads for integrity.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype="tlpt:independence" earliest=-400d conflict_flag=true
| stats latest(signed_date) as last_signed, values(attestation_type) as types by tester_id, engagement_id
| sort tester_id
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA TLPT — Tester Independence and Conflict-of-Interest Attestation Log** — Independence of testers from operational teams is a supervisory expectation. A tamper-evident log of tester employer changes, asset holdings declarations, and recusal events supports governance of TLPT quality.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` `sourcetype="tlpt:independence"` (tester_id, engagement_id, attestation_type, signed_date, conflict_flag). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): HTTP Event Collector from HR/GRC web form. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: tlpt:independence. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype="tlpt:independence", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by tester_id, engagement_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (conflicts), Single value (open conflicts), Pie by attestation_type.
